static int parseIntInternal (String str, int radix)

Detailed Description

Instances of class Integer represent primitive int values.

Additionally, this class provides various helper functions and variables related to ints.

Author:
Paul Fisher
John Keiser
Warren Levy
Eric Blake (ebb9@email.byu.edu)
Tom Tromey (tromey@redhat.com)

Definition at line 299 of file Integer.java.

static int java::lang::Integer::parseInt ( String  str,
int  radix 
) throws NumberFormatException [inline, static]

Converts the specified String into an int using the specified radix (base). The string must not be null or empty. It may begin with an optional '-', which will negate the answer, provided that there are also valid digits. Each digit is parsed as if by Character.digit(d, radix), and must be in the range 0 to radix - 1. Finally, the result must be within MIN_VALUE to MAX_VALUE, inclusive. Unlike Double.parseDouble, you may not have a leading '+'.

Parameters:
str the String to convert
radix the radix (base) to use in the conversion
Returns:
the String argument converted to int
Exceptions:
NumberFormatException if s cannot be parsed as an int

Definition at line 214 of file Integer.java.

References parseIntInternal().

static int java::lang::Integer::parseInt ( String  s  )  throws NumberFormatException [inline, static]

Converts the specified String into an int. This function assumes a radix of 10.

Parameters:
s the String to convert
Returns:
the int value of s
Exceptions:
NumberFormatException if s cannot be parsed as an int
See also:
parseInt(String, int)

int  radix

radix the radix to use
Returns:

Exceptions:

String java::lang::Integer::toString (  )  [inline]

Converts the Integer value to a String and assumes a radix of 10.

Returns:
the String representation

Definition at line 330 of file Integer.java.

References java::lang::String::valueOf().

static String java::lang::Integer::toString ( int  num,
int  radix 
) [inline, static]

Converts the int to a String using the specified radix (base). If the radix exceeds Character.MIN_RADIX or Character.MAX_RADIX, 10 is used instead. If the result is negative, the leading character is '-' ('\u002D'). The remaining characters come from Character.forDigit(digit, radix) ('0'-'9','a'-'z').

Parameters:
num the int to convert to String
radix the radix (base) to use in the conversion
Returns:
the String representation of the argument

Definition at line 108 of file Integer.java.

Parameters:

Returns:
the String representation of the argument
See also:
toString(int, int)

Field Documentation

final int java::lang::Integer::MAX_VALUE = 0x7fffffff [static]

The maximum value an int can represent is 2147483647 (or 231 - 1).

Definition at line 66 of file Integer.java.

Referenced by parseIntInternal().

final int java::lang::Integer::MIN_VALUE = 0x80000000 [static]

The minimum value an int can represent is -2147483648 (or -231).
